steps.title: Identify how ‘though’ functions in the sentence
steps.content: The word though is a supplementary transitional element inserted between the subject (“a signature of her work”) and the predicate (“she occasionally relies…”). A supplementary element like this must be enclosed by matching punctuation on both sides so it can be removed without breaking the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice that breaks the enclosure, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'work, though,': Comma before though and comma after though properly enclose the supplementary element. ✓
  • Choice B — 'work, though': Opens the enclosure with a comma but never closes it — the second comma is missing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'work ; though,': A semicolon cannot be paired with a comma to set off a supplementary element. ✗
  • Choice D — 'work, though ;': Opens with a comma but closes with a semicolon — the punctuation marks do not match. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
